## Summary

Redesigns the logged-in dashboard around the author progress ledger model we discussed: evaluation history, readiness movement, score trend, and clearly marked pending Revise analytics.

## What changed

- Adds `components/dashboard/AuthorProgressLedger.jsx`.
- Wires `/dashboard` to the new author progress ledger component.
- Keeps existing dashboard data source and KPI computation.
- Shows real evaluation history and current-manuscript score trend from existing saved evaluation rows.
- Keeps Revise analytics honest by labeling Must / Should / Good repair counts as pending until Revise queue/opportunity data is persisted.
- Keeps dashboard included/progress-ledger language: no extra charge to view progress.

## Scope

Dashboard UI/wiring only.

No database schema, no pricing runtime, no Revise queue persistence, no evaluation scoring, no pipeline, no worker, no Agent Readiness runtime, no Storygate runtime, no checkout, and no report-generation changes.

## Acceptance criteria

- `/dashboard` renders the author progress ledger layout.
- Existing evaluation history still displays.
- KPI cards still use existing dashboard evaluation data.
- Score Trend shows repeated evaluations for the current manuscript when available.
- Revise Analytics section clearly states it is pending Revise persistence and does not claim issue reduction yet.
- Dashboard remains included as progress visibility, not a separately monetized feature.

## Follow-up

Next PR should focus on Revise opportunity taxonomy/data shape: Must / Should / Good, repair scope, direct rewrite vs repair brief vs rewrite pass, and how those categories later feed dashboard charts.
